runtimeGuards: Article CTA hrefs use safe query params from router search params when available
Article CTA hrefs hydrate from stored first-touch attribution when router search params are unavailable
Article CTA hrefs hydrate from the current browser URL query when router search params and stored attribution are initially unavailable
Test detail and take URLs preserve safe UTM and SEO CTA context that arrived from an article CTA
Attribution capture preserves existing safe UTM fields across same-site navigation without adding PII
Article answer-surface test CTAs use SeoTrackedCtaLink when article source context is provided
Article CMS rich-content HTML test links are sanitized before DOM href attribution hydration
Article CMS Markdown-rendered test links are hydrated with the same safe DOM href attribution policy
Non-test CMS rich-content links remain unchanged
deferred: Backend-owned first-class attribution fields for source_slug, cta_id, and target_test_slug
Additional live acceptance for non-RIASEC article-to-test funnels
